Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Standard P2P Process Typical sequence 1 Create Purchase Requisition PR 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Issue Purchase Order PO 4 Receive Goods Services 5 Vendor Invoice Received 6 Match Invoice to PO and Goods Receipt 3 way match 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key controls rules Approval must happen before issuing a PO PO must be issued before receiving goods usually and before invoice matching Goods receipt should typically happen before or around the same time as invoice receipt but definitely before payment Invoice matching 3 way match PO GR Invoice must happen before payment release Payment should only be released after successful matching and verification Chronological order of timestamps must align with logical process flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Analysis Follows standard sequence perfectly No anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Analysis Major anomalies 1 PO issued before PR approval 2 Payment released before PR approval before goods receipt and before invoice matching 3 Invoice matching happens after payment 4 Goods received after payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This violates segregation of duties approval controls and 3 way matching High risk of fraud paying for undelivered goods or bypassing controls

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Analysis Major anomaly 1 Payment released before vendor invoice is received before goods are received and before invoice matching

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This completely bypasses the verification and matching controls High risk of overpayment paying for non existent goods or cash flow mismanagement

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Analysis Anomaly 1 Goods received before PO is issued

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a maverick buying or off contract procurement scenario It bypasses the formal ordering process potentially violating vendor contracts pricing agreements and budget controls

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Start with a brief overview of the standard P2P flow Go case by case or anomaly by anomaly highlighting the deviations For each anomaly explain What happened sequence timestamps Why it is anomalous vs standard practice Why it is problematic risks financial compliance fraud operational Provide a summary conclusion with recommendations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for completeness Did I cover all cases Yes Did I explain why anomalous Yes Did I explain why problematic Yes Grounded in P2P best practices Yes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Verification during thought Check timestamps for 1002 Yes retroactive Check 1003 Payment definitely before verification Check 1004 Goods before PO by 15 mins Still a violation All align with standard P2P control violations
